reason: The Moon is a satellite, not a planet
A wolf is not a big cat
A cube is 3D; the rest are 2D shapes
Diamond is not a metal
A flute is wind; the rest are string instruments
The Mediterranean is a sea, not an ocean
A shark is a fish; the rest are mammals
Copper is a metal; the rest are gases
Python is a programming language
A sphere is 3D
A bat is a mammal, not a bird

Chlorine is a halogen; the others top the periodic table

A gecko is a lizard, not a snake
Morocco is in Africa, not Europe
A triangle has three sides; the rest have four

Bamboo is a grass, not a tree
Granite is a rock, not a gemstone
Chess is not a physical sport
The femur is a bone, not an organ
A quasar is a distant galactic object
Brazil is in the Southern Hemisphere among these / not in Europe-Asia-Africa cluster
A trombone is brass; the rest are the string family
A triangle is the only one without an even-sounding 'gon' polygon name set / fewest sides
A lizard is a reptile; the rest are amphibians
The Moon reflects light; the rest emit or streak light
Toyota is a carmaker, not a tech company
A square has corners; the rest are curved
Titan is a moon, not a planet
Sunk cost is a cognitive bias; the others are game theory solution concepts
Monty Hall is a probability puzzle; the others are game theory games
Anchor is a cognitive bias; the others are strategies in game theory
Mixed strategy is a type of strategy; the others describe payoff structures
Arbitrage is an economic strategy; the others are cognitive biases
Adverse selection is an economic market failure; the others are cognitive/psychological biases
Dominant strategy is a game theory concept; the others are statistical phenomena
Nash Equilibrium is a game theory concept; the others are market structures
Arbitrage exploits price gaps; the others arise from information asymmetry problems
BATNA is a negotiation concept; the others are economics/market failure concepts
Prisoner's Dilemma is a game theory model; the others are negotiation concepts
Price elasticity is an economics measure; the others are strategic communication concepts
Nash Equilibrium is a game theory concept; the others are ethical frameworks
Dominant strategy is a game theory term; the others are moral philosophy concepts
Moral hazard is an economics concept; the others are statistical/probabilistic phenomena
Winner's curse is an auction economics concept; the others are classic probability paradoxes
Dominant Strategy is a game theory term; the others are blockchain consensus mechanisms
Nash Equilibrium is a game theory solution concept; the others are blockchain/crypto attacks
Sunk cost fallacy is a cognitive bias; the others are DeFi/trading strategies
Nash Equilibrium is a game theory concept; the others are physics principles
Merkle Tree is a cryptographic data structure used in blockchains; the others are foundational game theory concepts.
Slippery Slope is a logical fallacy; the other three are cognitive biases rooted in behavioral psychology.
Haskell, Erlang, and Clojure are primarily functional programming languages; Rust is a systems language emphasizing ownership and memory safety, not functional paradigm.
Neutron stars, white dwarfs, and black holes are stellar remnants (endpoints of stellar evolution); a quasar is an active galactic nucleus powered by a supermassive black hole, not a stellar remnant.
BFT is a distributed systems property/theorem; the other three are specific blockchain consensus mechanisms.
Planck's, Boltzmann's, and Avogadro's constants are dimensionless or fixed physical constants; the Hubble Constant is an observational cosmological parameter that changes over time (Hubble tension).
Gothic fiction, magical realism, and cyberpunk are genres defined by setting/atmosphere/tropes; Bildungsroman is a narrative structure (coming-of-age arc) that can span any genre.
Ad hominem, straw man, and false dilemma are logical fallacies; Occam's Razor is a philosophical principle of parsimony used to guide reasoning.
Keynes, Friedman, and Hayek are foundational macroeconomists; Euler is a mathematician whose contributions are in analysis and graph theory, not economics.
Galileo, Newton, and Descartes were 17th-century Scientific Revolution figures; Tesla was a 19th–20th century electrical engineer, a different era entirely.
ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 are Ethereum token standards; BEP-2 is a Binance Chain token standard, from a different ecosystem.
Availability, representativeness, and affect are heuristics (mental shortcuts); Dunning-Kruger is a metacognitive bias about self-assessment of competence, not a heuristic.
Crab, Orion, and Eagle nebulae are nebulae within the Milky Way; Andromeda is a full spiral galaxy 2.5 million light-years away.
Dominant strategy, mixed strategy, and the minimax theorem are solution concepts/strategies in game theory; zero-sum game is a game classification, not a strategy or theorem.
Lisp, ML, and APL are functional or array-oriented languages; Prolog is a logic programming language based on Horn clause resolution.
Speed of light, gravitational constant, and elementary charge are physical constants with units and empirical values; Pi is a pure mathematical constant.
Vitalik Buterin, Gavin Wood, and Nick Szabo are identified individuals with verifiable public histories; Satoshi Nakamoto is a pseudonymous unknown identity.
Stream of consciousness, unreliable narrator, and in medias res are narrative techniques; epistolary is a literary form (works told through letters/documents), not just a technique.
Comparative advantage, opportunity cost, and marginal utility are core microeconomic/trade concepts; Nash Equilibrium belongs to game theory.
Plato, Aristotle, and Socrates form the classical Athenian philosophical lineage; Confucius is a Chinese philosopher from a separate tradition and slightly earlier era.
SHA-256, Keccak-256, and RIPEMD-160 are cryptographic hash functions; AES-256 is a symmetric encryption algorithm, not a hash function.
Confirmation bias, in-group bias, and optimism bias are cognitive biases; modus ponens is a deductive logical inference rule.

Pulsars, magnetars, and neutron stars are all closely related compact stellar objects (magnetars and pulsars are subtypes of neutron stars); a blazar is an active galactic nucleus with a relativistic jet pointed at Earth.
Prisoner's Dilemma, Chicken Game, and Stag Hunt are canonical two-player game theory games; Condorcet Paradox is a social choice theory result about voting cycles.
Scala, F#, and OCaml are functional/multi-paradigm languages with strong type systems and ML lineage; Go is an imperative, concurrency-focused systems language.
Gravitational constant, permittivity of free space, and Boltzmann constant are dimensional constants; the fine-structure constant is dimensionless (approximately 1/137).
Mary Shelley, Poe, and Ann Radcliffe are foundational Gothic/horror fiction authors; Dickens was a social realist novelist who occasionally used Gothic elements but is not a Gothic author.
Solidity, Vyper, and Move are smart contract programming languages designed for blockchain VMs; Rust is a general-purpose systems language (though used in some blockchain tooling).
Ad populum, tu quoque, and appeal to authority are informal logical fallacies; reductio ad absurdum is a valid proof technique (showing a premise leads to contradiction).
Adam Smith, David Ricardo, and John Stuart Mill are classical economists; Thomas Bayes was an 18th-century statistician and Presbyterian minister known for Bayes' theorem.
UTXO, Account Model, and State Channel are blockchain transaction/ledger models; Merkle Patricia Trie is a data structure (used internally in Ethereum) not a transaction model.

Overconfidence effect, hindsight bias, and false consensus effect are stable perceptual/memory biases; the planning fallacy is a specific prediction bias about underestimating task duration, studied distinctly by Kahneman and Tversky.

Cepheid variables, RR Lyrae stars, and T Tauri stars are types of variable stars at different evolutionary stages; Type Ia supernova is a stellar explosion event used as a standard candle, not a type of variable star.
Mechanism design, auction theory, and signaling theory are branches of game theory/information economics; portfolio theory (Markowitz) is a quantitative finance model for asset allocation.
Idris, Agda, and Coq are dependently-typed proof assistants/languages used in formal verification; Erlang is a concurrent functional language designed for fault-tolerant telecom systems.
Speed of light in vacuum, magnetic constant, and gas constant are universal physical constants; speed of sound in air is a material-dependent property that varies with temperature and pressure.

Borges, Calvino, and Kafka are associated with magical realism and metafiction in a postmodern/surrealist vein; García Márquez is specifically tied to Latin American magical realism and is the most canonical example — yet the odd one out is Kafka, whose work predates magical realism as a named movement and is more properly Kafkaesque/surrealist. Actually: Kafka is the odd one — the other three are post-WWII authors; Kafka died 1924.
Liquidity pools, AMMs, and yield farming are DeFi-native constructs; order books are traditional finance market mechanisms used in centralized exchanges.

Hindsight bias, curse of knowledge, and illusion of transparency all involve overestimating what others know or what was predictable; base rate neglect is a statistical reasoning error about prior probabilities.
Io, Europa, and Ganymede are moons of Jupiter (Galilean moons); Titan is a moon of Saturn.
Pareto efficiency, Kaldor-Hicks efficiency, and Arrow's Impossibility Theorem are welfare economics/social choice concepts; Rawlsian Justice is a political philosophy framework from John Rawls.
Erlang, Elixir, and Pony are programming languages with native actor-model/concurrency support; Akka is a toolkit/library for JVM (Scala/Java), not a language itself.

Chandrasekhar limit, TOV limit, and Schwarzschild radius are mass/density thresholds for compact object formation; Eddington luminosity is a limit on radiation pressure vs. gravity, not a mass/collapse threshold.
Rollups, sidechains, and Plasma are Layer-2 scaling solutions that offload transactions from the main chain; sharding is a Layer-1 scaling technique that partitions the base blockchain itself.

Tversky, Kahneman, and Thaler are behavioral economists/psychologists who founded behavioral economics; Stiglitz is an information economist known for asymmetric information theory, not behavioral economics.
Sonnet, villanelle, and haiku are poetic forms defined by strict structural rules; elegy is a mood/subject category (mourning), not a fixed structural form.

Dark matter, dark energy, and the cosmological constant are theoretical entities/parameters accounting for unobserved mass-energy; the CMB is an observed electromagnetic radiation phenomenon — empirical evidence, not a theoretical placeholder.
Circular economy, tragedy of the commons, and externality are environmental/public goods economics concepts; comparative advantage is a trade theory concept.

Curry, Church, and Turing are foundational theorists of computability and lambda calculus; von Neumann's key contribution was the stored-program computer architecture, distinct from computability theory foundations.
EIP-1559, EIP-4844, and EIP-4337 are Ethereum Improvement Proposals; BIP-32 is a Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (hierarchical deterministic wallets).
Mental accounting, hyperbolic discounting, and status quo bias are behavioral/cognitive biases; moral hazard is an information economics concept about risk-taking when insulated from consequences.
Rhea, Dione, and Tethys are moons of Saturn; Callisto is a Galilean moon of Jupiter.
Dominant strategy equilibrium, subgame perfect equilibrium, and Bayesian Nash equilibrium are solution concepts in game theory; the Efficient Market Hypothesis belongs to financial economics.
Simula, Smalltalk, and Self are historically pivotal object-oriented languages; ALGOL is a procedural/imperative language that influenced structured programming, not OOP directly.
Electron, proton, and neutron masses are measured particle masses from the Standard Model; Planck mass is a derived unit from fundamental constants (combination of G, hbar, c), not a particle mass.
Canterbury Tales, Gargantua, and Decameron are medieval/early Renaissance frame narrative collections; Don Quixote (1605) is widely considered the first modern novel, a distinct literary form.
ZK proofs, homomorphic encryption, and MPC are privacy-preserving computation techniques; digital signatures are an authentication mechanism, not a privacy-preserving computation technique.

Framing effect, anchoring effect, and mere exposure effect are cognitive biases affecting judgment and preference; priming is a memory/perception mechanism (not strictly a bias) studied in cognitive psychology, foundational to social priming research.
Gamma-ray bursts, fast radio bursts, and gravitational waves are extragalactic or deep-space phenomena; solar flares originate from our own Sun and are local space weather events.
Coase theorem, Pigou tax, and Hotelling's rule are models/theorems in environmental/resource economics; the Laffer Curve is a supply-side fiscal policy concept about tax rates and revenue.
Forth, C, and BASIC are high-level or mid-level programming languages; Assembly is a low-level language that directly maps to machine instructions with no abstraction layer.
Starknet, zkSync, and Optimism are Ethereum Layer-2 rollup networks; Polygon PoS is a sidechain (not a rollup) with its own validator set separate from Ethereum's security.

Loss aversion, risk aversion, and ambiguity aversion are all decision-under-uncertainty preferences studied in prospect theory; cognitive dissonance is a motivational state arising from holding contradictory beliefs.

Borromean rings, trefoil knot, and Hopf fibration are topological structures in 3-dimensional space; a Calabi-Yau manifold is a complex higher-dimensional manifold used in string theory compactification.

Petrarch, Dante, and Boccaccio are the three crowns of Italian literature from 14th-century Florence; Chaucer is their English contemporary who adapted Italian forms but belongs to the English literary tradition.
Asymmetric information, principal-agent problem, and adverse selection are information economics concepts; a Giffen good is a demand anomaly in consumer theory where demand rises with price.
Kotlin, Swift, and Dart are statically typed languages primarily targeting native mobile/app development; TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript targeting web/browser environments.

Hawking radiation, Unruh effect, and Casimir effect are quantum vacuum/field theory phenomena with no classical analog; Cherenkov radiation is a classical electromagnetic effect from charged particles exceeding light speed in a medium.

Reentrancy attacks, front-running, and sandwich attacks are attack vectors/exploits on DeFi protocols; flash loans are a legitimate (though dual-use) uncollateralized lending mechanism native to DeFi.
Framing effect, affect heuristic, and decoy effect are individual cognitive phenomena; social proof is a social influence mechanism (Cialdini) where behavior is shaped by others' actions.

Io, Enceladus, and Titan are moons known for geological/cryovolcanic activity; Triton is a retrograde moon of Neptune likely captured from the Kuiper Belt, distinct by its origin and orbital characteristics.
Shapley value, core solution, and nucleolus are solution concepts in cooperative game theory; minimax regret is a decision criterion in decision theory under uncertainty, not cooperative game theory.

APL, J, and K are array programming languages in the Iverson lineage (J and K are direct descendants of APL); R is a statistical computing language that supports array operations but is not in the APL/Iverson family.
Stefan-Boltzmann, Wien displacement, and Rydberg constants all arise from quantum/spectral physics of radiation; the molar gas constant R is a thermodynamic constant from the ideal gas law.

Hard boiled, Southern Gothic, and noir are American literary/genre traditions; Gothic fiction is a European Romantic-era tradition (Walpole, Radcliffe, Shelley) that precedes and differs from Southern Gothic's American regional variant.
Tokenomics, vesting schedules, and token burns are crypto-native economic mechanisms; the Capital Asset Pricing Model is a traditional finance model for pricing risk and return.

Groupthink, bandwagon effect, and information cascade are social dynamics phenomena; availability cascade is a self-reinforcing process where repeated media coverage makes a risk seem larger — a hybrid of media theory and cognitive bias.
Oort Cloud, Kuiper Belt, and the Asteroid Belt are regions of small bodies in the solar system; the Van Allen Belts are zones of trapped charged particles in Earth's magnetosphere.
Vickrey, Dutch, and English auctions are single-sided auction formats; a double auction has both buyers and sellers submitting bids/asks simultaneously (used in stock markets).
Whitespace, Brainfuck, and Malbolge are esoteric programming languages (esolangs) designed for fun/obfuscation; PostScript is a practical page description language used in professional printing.
Penrose process, frame-dragging (Lense-Thirring effect), and gravitational lensing are predictions of general relativity; quantum entanglement is a quantum mechanical phenomenon not predicted by GR.
DAOs, multi-sig wallets, and smart contracts are on-chain programmable constructs; cold wallets are offline key storage devices with no on-chain programmability.
Endowment effect, IKEA effect, and mere ownership effect are all biases related to overvaluing things one possesses or creates; the Zeigarnik effect is about better memory for uncompleted tasks.

Proxima Centauri, Barnard's Star, and Alpha Centauri A are among the nearest stars to the Sun; Sirius, while the brightest star in the night sky, is approximately 8.6 light-years away — farther than the others listed.

Stackelberg, Bertrand, and Cournot are strategic oligopoly models from game theory (with defined best-response dynamics); monopolistic competition is a market structure concept from industrial organization, not a strategic game.

Tcl, Lua, and Ruby were designed primarily as scripting/embeddable languages with an emphasis on extensibility and integration; Python was designed with readability and general-purpose use as primary goals.
Big Crunch, Heat Death, and Big Rip are proposed end-states of the universe; cosmic inflation is a proposed early-universe rapid expansion phase, not an end-state.

Token curated registries, bonding curves, and conviction voting are DeFi/DAO governance and curation mechanisms; Proof of Humanity is an identity verification protocol, not a curation/governance mechanism.

Cognitive load theory, dual-process theory (System 1/2), and signal detection theory are cognitive psychology frameworks; Terror Management Theory is a social psychology theory about mortality salience and cultural worldviews.
L1, L2, and L4 are Lagrange points (gravitational equilibrium positions in a two-body system); barycenter is the center of mass of a system, a different concept entirely.

Tiebout model, median voter theorem, and public choice theory are political economy models; the Gibbard-Satterthwaite theorem is a social choice/mechanism design impossibility result about strategy-proofness of voting systems.

FORTRAN, PL/I, and ALGOL 60 heavily influenced the development of structured/scientific programming and later languages; COBOL was designed specifically for business data processing and has a distinct English-like syntax lineage.

Gravitino, photino, and neutralino are supersymmetric partner particles (superpartners) predicted by SUSY; the inflaton is a hypothetical scalar field that drove cosmic inflation, not a SUSY particle.
Cosmos SDK, Hardhat, and Foundry are all Ethereum or EVM-related development frameworks/toolkits; Substrate is a Polkadot/Parity framework for building custom blockchains independent of EVM.

Moral licensing, virtue signaling, and moral disengagement are mechanisms by which people justify unethical behavior or project ethical identity; fundamental attribution error is a social cognition error about over-attributing behavior to character over situation.

Globular clusters, open clusters, and OB associations are gravitationally bound or co-formed stellar groups; stellar streams are tidal debris from disrupted clusters or dwarf galaxies, not a bound structure.

Revelation principle, folk theorem, and Myerson-Satterthwaite theorem are core theorems in mechanism design and game theory; the envelope theorem is a mathematical result in optimization/comparative statics used across economics.
CockroachDB, TiDB, and Google Spanner are distributed NewSQL databases with ACID guarantees across nodes; Redis is an in-memory key-value store/cache, not a distributed relational database.

Tit-for-Tat, Grim Trigger, and Mixed Strategy Nash are strategies or equilibria in repeated game theory; Defect-Cooperate is simply a description of action outcomes in a single game matrix, not a strategy name.
Slashing, jailing, and tombstoning are validator punishment mechanisms in Proof-of-Stake consensus protocols; forking is a protocol upgrade/split mechanism, not a validator penalty.

Tokyo is in Japan; the rest are in Southeast/East Asia excluding Japan
Bismuth is the heaviest stable element; the rest are unstable/radioactive
Sagrada Família is still under construction; the rest are ancient structures
Graviton is hypothetical/unobserved; the rest are confirmed force carriers
Pythagoras lived in ancient Greece; the rest were 18th-19th century mathematicians
Tamil is a Dravidian language; the rest are Austronesian languages
Nietzsche philosophized after the German Idealist era; the rest were German Idealists
Aluminium is not a platinum group metal; the rest are PGMs
Hinduism originated in South Asia; the rest originated in East/Northeast Asia

Shoemaker-Levy 9 impacted Jupiter; the rest are periodic comets
Ceres is a dwarf planet; the others are planets.

Basque is a language isolate; the others are Romance languages.
Nitrogen is not a noble gas; the others are.
Kilimanjaro is in Africa; the others are Himalayan eight-thousanders.
Copper is not ferromagnetic; the others are.
A trapezoid has only one pair of parallel sides; the others are parallelograms.
A penguin is a bird; the others are mammals.
Carthage is an ancient ruined city; the others are modern capitals.
Water is a compound; the others are elements.
Chess is a board game; the others are physical sports.
Sodium is solid at room temperature; the others are liquid.
Herodotus was a historian; the others were mathematicians.
Quartz is silicon dioxide; the others are pure carbon allotropes.
Vulcan is a hypothetical planet that does not exist; the others are real.
Europa is a moon of Jupiter; the others are planets.
The Sahara is a desert; the others are rivers.
The falcon can fly; the others are flightless birds.
Switzerland is landlocked; the others border the Atlantic or a sea.
Bangkok is in Thailand (Southeast Asia); the others are East Asian capitals.
Pluto is a dwarf planet; the others are gas/ice giants.
The potato is a tuber (root); the others are botanically fruits.
Cairo is in Africa; the others are European capitals.
Quartz is made of silicon and oxygen; the others are forms of carbon.
The wolf is a canine; the others are big cats.
Kilimanjaro is a free-standing volcano; the others are in mountain ranges.
London is a city; the others are currencies.
Baikal is a lake; the others are rivers.
Tolstoy was Russian; the others were British/English writers.
The bat is a mammal; the others are birds.
Water is a compound; the others are metallic elements.
Japan is an island nation in the Eastern Hemisphere; the others are among the world's largest countries by area.
Soccer is played chiefly with the feet; the others are played with the hands or a hand-held implement.
German is a Germanic language; the others are Romance languages.
The circle has no straight sides; the others are polygons.
Odin is a Norse god; the others are Greek gods.
The Concorde was an aircraft; the others were ships.
Bronze is an alloy; the others are gaseous elements.
The Amazon is a rainforest; the others are deserts.
The Sun is a star; the others are planets.
The lizard is a reptile; the others are amphibians.
Beethoven was a composer; the others were Renaissance painters.
Ben Nevis is not a volcano; the others are volcanoes.
Lettuce is a leafy vegetable; the others are cereal grains.
The Caspian is a sea/lake; the others are oceans.
The giraffe is not classed among the heavyweight thick-skinned pachyderms; the others are pachyderms.
Poker is a card game; the others are board games.
A pearl is organic (formed in a mollusc); the others are mineral gemstones.
Finland is not part of the Scandinavian Peninsula and its language is non-Germanic; the others are Scandinavian in the strict sense.
A constellation is a pattern of stars; the others are small solar-system bodies.
The octopus is a mollusc; the others are fish.
Churchill was British; the others were U.S. presidents.
The Volga flows entirely within one country (Russia) into an inland sea; the others flow to the ocean/North Sea.
The pine is a coniferous evergreen; the others are deciduous broadleaf trees.
Hamlet is a play; the others are paintings.
The anaconda is a non-venomous constrictor; the others are venomous snakes.
Big Ben is a modern (19th-century) structure; the others are ancient wonders/monuments.
Portugal is on the European mainland; the others are large islands.
Venus is an inner terrestrial planet; the others are outer giant planets.
The spider is an arachnid with eight legs; the others are insects with six.
Hindi is an Indo-European language; the others are East Asian languages of the Sino-Tibetan or Japonic families spoken in East Asia.
Mick Jagger is from the Rolling Stones; the others were Beatles.
Beethoven was a composer; the others were painters.
Hungarian is not a Romance language; the others descend from Latin.
Gladiator was directed by Ridley Scott; the others were directed by James Cameron.
Mercury is a liquid metal at room temperature; the others are gases.
Australia is a continent; the others are islands.
Sydney is not a national capital; the others are capital cities.
A spider has eight legs and is an arachnid; the others are insects.
A guitar is typically plucked with frets; but more precisely the violin, cello, and harp are all orchestral strings while the guitar is not standard in an orchestra.
Uranus is named after a Greek god; Mars, Venus, and Jupiter are named after Roman gods.
A wolf is a canine; the others are big cats.
Iron is a solid metal; the others are major gases in Earth's atmosphere.
Freddie Mercury was primarily a band frontman (Queen); the others were chiefly solo pop stars.
Mumbai is not a national capital; the others are capital cities.
Gravity is a physical force; the others are biological processes.
A circle has no straight sides; the others are quadrilaterals.
Picasso was a painter; the others were classical composers.
The Moon reflects light and is not a star; the others are stars.
Boeing makes aircraft; the others are car manufacturers.
An eagle can fly; the others are flightless birds.
The Eiffel Tower is a modern structure; the others are ancient constructions.
Mercury is a pure element; the others are alloys.
Oliver Twist was written by Dickens; the others are Shakespeare plays.
Calcium is an alkaline earth metal; the others are alkali metals.
Bob Marley was a reggae artist; the others were early rock and roll pioneers.
A bat is a mammal; the others are birds.
A rainbow is an optical phenomenon; the others are geological or seismic events.
Latin is a dead language no longer natively spoken; the others are living languages.
Earth is a rocky terrestrial planet; the others are gas or ice giants.
Rembrandt was a Dutch Golden Age painter; the others were Italian Renaissance masters.
Tchaikovsky was a Romantic-era composer; the others belong to the Baroque or Classical eras.
The Danube is a European river; the others flow through Africa.
Water is a liquid; the others are solids at room temperature.
A satellite (like the Moon) orbits a planet; the others travel through space independently.
A cheetah is a wild cat; the others are dog breeds.
A carrot is a root vegetable; the others are fruits.
